themes.label: Salvation
themes.summary: This chapter speaks of rescue, deliverance, redemption, life, or being saved. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 1 verse. Cross references echo "Redemption".

themes.label: Kingdom
themes.summary: This chapter stresses reign, kingdom, throne, rule, or the authority of God and His Christ. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 1 verse. Commentary leans on "And they brought the head of Ish-bosheth unto David to Hebron, and said to the king, Behold t...". Word-study detail reinforces "הַמֶּ֤לֶךְ: the king - a king".

themes.label: Creation
themes.summary: This chapter underscores creation, the heavens and earth, or God’s creative ordering work. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 1 verse. Commentary leans on "How much more, when wicked men have slain a righteous person in his own house upon his bed? s...". Word-study detail reinforces "הָאָֽרֶץ׃: from the earth - the earth (at large, or partitively a land)".

themes.label: Peace/Rest
themes.summary: This chapter offers peace, rest, stillness, comfort, or settled safety in God.

evidenceTrail.label: Verse 5 word study
evidenceTrail.detail: שֹׁכֵ֔ב: who lay - to lie down (for rest, sexual connection, decease or any other purpose)

themes.label: Suffering
themes.summary: This chapter deals with affliction, sorrow, trial, lament, endurance, or hardship.
